rulings.Case Title: KEVIN QUILLINAN et al VS. NEW COLLEGE OF LAW et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 28, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANTS PETER GABEL, ED ROYBAL, AND, CHRIS KANIOS DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. As to first, third, fourth and fifth causes of action, plaintiff offers no specifics as to how amendment could cure these claims. As to second cause of action, second amended complaint alleges that false representations occurred "on or about the beginning of the 2004 school year" and that accreditation was lost about April 2007. No false statement made to support a fraud claim. No sufficient duty alleged on the part of the demurring defendants to disclose New College's alleged tenuous standing with the WASC. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: YEONG JOO KIM VS. DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, AS TRUSTEE et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 28, 2010, line 5, DEFENDANT ONEWEST BANK, FSB, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. The first cause of action for declaratory relief is inconsistent in that attachment A complies with Civil Code section 2923.5. This mortgage for real property does not state a cause of action under Civil Code section 1632, and no exceptions are alleged. No fiduciary relationship properly alleged in second cause of action. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN GREEN VS. MARK FEATHERS, ET AL.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 28, 2010, line 7, DEFENDANTS MARK FEATHERS, INVESTORS PRIME FUND, LLC DEMURRER. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff may clarify the allegations against each defendant, and, as to the fraud claim, must plead the elements of fraud with particularity. Plaintiff may add a new defendant. Defendants' objections to that amendment may be raised separately once the amended complaint is filed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ST. ANTHONY FOUNDATION VS. SCM ADVISORS, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Scm Advisors, Llc, Virtus Investment Partners, Inc., And Diane Spirandelli'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings (Transferred From Dept. 301, Per Recusal Of Judge Busch)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 28, 2010, line 12, Defendants SCM Advisors, LLC, Virtus Investment Partners, Inc., and Diane Spirandelli's Motion To Compel Arbitration and Stay Proceedings (Transferred From Dept. 301, Per Recusal Of Judge Busch). Motion granted. FAA appears to apply, and the last sentence of the arbitration clause does not include any language that is specific enough to justify disregarding the FAA. Motion also granted under California law. Although the claims against the CGMI and the SCM defendants arise from the same transactions, the claims are quite different and any prejudice that may arise from considering the disputes in two separate forums is minor. California law favors arbitrability, and the risk of inconsistent rulings is slight. CGMI action is stayed until the SCM - Foundation arbitration is complete. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN C POH JR RN NP VS. BOARD OF REGISTERED NURSING DEPARTMENT OF CONSUMER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petitioner John C. Poh, Jr.'S Petition For Writ Of Mandate (Per Stipulation & Order Of 10/27/09)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 28, 2010, line 9, Petitioner John C. Poh, Jr.'s Petition For Writ Of Mandate (Per Stipulation & Order Of 10/27/09). Petition is granted. Respondent did comply initially with G.C. sec. 11517(c)(2)(A) because it did "adopt" the ALJ's decision within the 100 day period. Furthermore, CCP sec. 1008 reconsideration requirements are not applicable to G.C. sec. 11521 which provides more leeway to an agency. However, the court must reconcile the language of G.C. sec. 11517 and 11521. In respondent's 10-31-08 order, it "reconsidered" its prior adoption of the ALJ's ruling, vacated that decision, and ordered that the agency must itself determine petitioner's case. That order was a de facto rejection and agency decision to decide the case itself. Accordingly, the 100-day timing provision of G.C. sec. 11517(c)(2)(E)(iv) should apply. This interpretation upholds the policies highlighted in St. Francis Medical Center vs. Shewry (2005) 134 Cal.App. 4th 1556 and Matus vs. Bd. of Admin. of CALPERS (2009) 177 Cal.App.4th 597. As to the substantive issues, the court's standard of review is independent judgment, affording a strong presumption of correctness concerning the administrative findings. The challenging party bears the burden of convincing the court that the administrative findings are contrary to the weight of the evidence. Fukuda vs. City of Angels (1999) 20 Cal. 4th 805. Petitioner has met this burden. Petitioner's experts were exceptionally qualified. While the standard of review may be the same in a correctional and noncorrectional setting, Lobitz's lack of any correctionsal experience weighs against her. It is clar that petitioner believed that certain testing would not be necessary because E.M. was going to be released. Lobitz did not sufficiently explain why she changed her reports. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ANTIQUE & SALVAGE LIQUIDATORS, INC. VS. STATE OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, January 28, 2010, line 10, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA INTEGRATED WASTE MANAGEMENT BOARD DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION. Demurrer overruled in part and sustained in part, with ten days leave to amend. As to first, second and fifth causes of action petitioners failed to plead compliance with the Tort Claims Act. As to the fourth cause of action, petitioner must plead the nature of the taking. Demurrer overruled as to punitive damages claim that is properly subject to a motion to strike, not a demurrer. =(302/CWW)
